title: Bean There, Done That Day!
story: Every July 3, bean lovers across America celebrate National Eat Beans Day by loading up on their favorite legumes! People enjoy black beans in tacos, kidney beans in chili, and green beans as healthy sides. Restaurants offer special bean dishes, while families share recipes passed down through generations. Some folks even host bean cook-offs to see who makes the tastiest creation!
activity_question: What do some people host on National Eat Beans Day to see who makes the best dish?
bonus_fact: There are over 400 types of beans grown around the world!

title: Hide and Seek from the Sun
story: July 3 is National Stay Out of the Sun Day! People celebrate by finding shade, wearing hats, and staying indoors during the hottest hours to protect their skin.
activity_question: What do people wear on National Stay Out of the Sun Day to protect themselves?
bonus_fact: Your shadow is shortest when the sun's rays are strongest!

title: Cool Down and Celebrate AC Day!
story: Every July 3, people across America celebrate National Air Conditioning Appreciation Day by cranking up their AC units and enjoying the cool breeze indoors! Families gather in their chilliest rooms, restaurants advertise their "arctic" dining experiences, and social media fills with photos of people hugging their air conditioners. It's the perfect summer holiday to beat the heat and thank the amazing invention that keeps us comfortable during scorching weather.
activity_question: How do people typically celebrate National Air Conditioning Appreciation Day?
activity_choices: By staying in cool, air-conditioned spaces
By turning off all their electronics
By having outdoor barbecues in the sun
activity_answer: By staying in cool, air-conditioned spaces
bonus_fact: The first modern air conditioner was invented in 1902 by Willis Carrier!

title: Wafer-ly Wonderful Chocolate Day!
story: Crispy, crunchy chocolate wafers get their own special day on July 3! These thin, layered cookies are perfect for dunking in milk, crushing up for ice cream toppings, or just munching straight from the package. Bakeries and candy shops celebrate by creating special wafer treats and sharing recipes online. Some families have wafer-eating contests to see who can bite through the most layers without breaking them. Others use chocolate wafers to build edible towers or make homemade ice cream sandwiches. Social media fills with photos of creative wafer creations and childhood memories of these beloved treats.
activity_question: What do some families do with chocolate wafers to make the day more fun?
bonus_fact: Chocolate wafers were first made in the 1800s as a fancy dessert for wealthy families.

title: Stuffed Guardians Get Their Day
story: Every July 3, families across the country celebrate Build a Scarecrow Day by creating their own stuffed garden guardians! People gather old clothes, straw, newspapers, and anything soft to stuff their scarecrows. Some make traditional farm-style protectors with overalls and floppy hats, while others get creative with colorful outfits and funny faces. Kids love drawing silly expressions and adding wild accessories like sunglasses or bow ties. Many families display their finished scarecrows in gardens, front yards, or porches to show off their handiwork. It's the perfect summer activity that combines creativity with a bit of old-fashioned farm tradition!
activity_question: What do people commonly use to stuff their scarecrows on Build a Scarecrow Day?
follow_up_question: What creative accessories would you add to make your scarecrow unique?

title: Clam Up and Chow Down Day!
story: Crispy, golden fried clams sizzle in restaurants across America every July 3 for National Fried Clam Day! These chewy ocean treats get dunked in batter and deep-fried until they're crunchy on the outside and tender inside. Seafood lovers head to coastal diners and clam shacks to munch on whole belly clams or clam strips with tartar sauce. Some people make them at home, while others post photos of their clam platters on social media. It's the perfect summer celebration for anyone who loves the taste of the sea!
activity_question: What do people dip fried clams in when they eat them?
bonus_fact: Whole belly clams include the soft, sweet belly part that many people consider the best bite!
